includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database

contaminant.name: Rubber Compound Residue
contaminant.description: Rubber-residue contamination arises from vulcanized rubber deposits of tire marks, seals, and gaskets, so it forms during friction and compression on surfaces. This residue shows unique elasticity and strong adhesion that distinguish it from oily or powdery contaminants because of its polymeric nature. On materials like steel or concrete, residue clings tightly and resists scraping, so removal challenges include incomplete cleaning without damaging substrate, yet laser cleaning succeeds by vaporizing layers precisely during pulsed exposure.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Hydrogen Cyanide, Benzene - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Benzene, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (15 ACH) for particulate control
